news.summary: In Dublin, a report titled "Push to Talk Market - Global Industry Size, Share, Trends, Opportunity, and Forecast, 2020-2030F" has now been included in ResearchAndMarkets.com's offerings. The Push to Talk Market achieved a value of USD 35.11 Billion in 2024 and is projected to hit USD 63.11 Billion by 2030, with a growth rate (CAGR) of 10.19%. The market is experiencing substantial expansion driven by increasing needs for immediate and dependable communication in sectors such as public safety, transportation, and more.
news.headline: Push to Talk Market projected to reach $63.11 Billion by 2030 with a 10.19% CAGR, increasing from $35.11 Billion in 2024.

news.summary: BrightView Holdings, based in Blue Bell, Pa., disclosed the pricing details of an underwritten secondary offering by a selling stockholder associated with KKR & Co. Inc. A total of 11,600,000 shares of BrightView's common stock will be offered in this transaction, as stated in the registration statement filed with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC). Notably, BrightView itself is not selling any shares in this offering. The entirety of the proceeds from the sale will go to the Selling Stockholder.
news.headline: BrightView's secondary offering priced at $11,600,000 shares by KKR & Co. Inc., no shares sold by BrightView.

news.summary: A company known as GEO Group, Inc. (NYSE: GEO) based in Boca Raton, Florida, has revealed a recent deal with the Oklahoma Department of Corrections to sell the Lawton Correctional Facility, situated in Lawton, Oklahoma. The purchase agreement stands at a substantial $312 million. The transaction is anticipated to be finalized on July 25, 2025, pending the fulfillment of standard closing requirements. GEO is preparing to transfer operational control of the Facility as part of this agreement.
news.headline: GEO Group to sell Lawton Correctional Facility in Oklahoma for $312 million to Oklahoma DOC, closing on July 25, 2025.

news.summary: Dollar General, located in Goodlettsville, Tennessee, is excited to announce the addition of celebrity brands to their inventory. This new offering aims to bring both style and value to homes across the country. This move is set to enhance the shopping experience for customers, providing access to exclusive and high-quality products. With this initiative, Dollar General continues to cater to its customer base, offering a diverse range of products to meet various needs.
news.headline: Dollar General is bringing celebrity brands to add style and value to every home in Goodlettsville, Tennessee.

news.summary: Alphawave Semi (LSE: AWE), a prominent player in high-speed connectivity and compute silicon for global technology infrastructure, recently disclosed the completion of the tape out for one of the industry's pioneering UCIe™ IP subsystems on TSMC's N2 process. This advancement enables support for impressive 36G die-to-die data rates. The integration includes TSMC's advanced packaging technology, Chip-on-Wafer-on-Substrate (CoWoS®), promising remarkable bandwidth density and scalability for future generations.
news.headline: Alphawave Semi unveils 36G UCIe™ IP on TSMC 2nm, boosting AI platform with CoWoS® tech. Breakthrough in data rates.

news.summary: Akron, Ohio-based company B&W has announced its intention to buy back up to $70 million worth of Tender Consideration through cash offers.
news.headline: Babcock & Wilcox initiates cash tender offer for two note series, aimed at $70 million aggregate amount.

news.summary: Lands' End, Inc. announced its financial results for the first quarter ended May 2, 2025. Gross Merchandise Value increased slightly excluding prior year impact, Net revenue decreased to $261.2 million, U.S. Digital Segment net revenue was $227.7 million, U.S. eCommerce increased to $170.7 million, Outfitters net revenue increased to $42.9 million, Third Party net revenue decreased to $14.1 million, Europe eCommerce net revenue was $17.9 million which indicates customer growth. Gross profit decreased to $132.7 million, Gross margin increased to 50.8%, Net loss was $8.3 million, Adjusted net loss was $5.4 million, and Adjusted EBITDA was $9.5 million. Cash and cash equivalents were $18.1 million, Inventories decreased to $262.4 million. The Company announced plans to explore strategic alternatives, including a potential sale. The outlook for fiscal 2025 includes Gross Merchandise Value growth, Net revenue between $1.33 billion and $1.45 billion, Net income between $8.0 million and $20.0 million, and adjusted net income between $15.0 million and $27.0 million. The company also plans for Adjusted EBITDA in the range of $95.0 million to $107.0 million. Full story available on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: Lands' End reports Q1 2025 results: GMV up, net revenue down $24.3M, adjusted EBITDA $9.5M, $18.1M cash on hand. Strategic alternatives process initiated. Forecast for fiscal 2025 unchanged.

news.summary: In an announcement made on June 5, 2025, Knight Therapeutics Inc. and Sumitomo Pharma America Inc. disclosed their exclusive license and supply agreements for commercializing MYFEMBREE ®, ORGOVYX ®, and vibegron in Canada. Additionally, Knight will acquire certain mature products alongside this deal for an undisclosed sum. These agreements are set to propel the pan-American specialty pharmaceutical company’s growth, especially after the Products amassed an impressive revenue of C$11.2 million for the year ended March 2025.
news.headline: Knight Therapeutics and Sumitomo Pharma partner to license and commercialize Sumitomo’s products in Canada, generating C$11.2 million revenue in 2025.

news.summary: Ongoing growth in Gross Merchandise Value (GMV) resulted in an increase of approximately 210 basis points in Gross Margin. Inventory was reduced for the eighth consecutive quarter.
news.headline: Lands’ End Q1 2025: GMV growth, +210 bps in Gross margin, 8th consecutive quarter inventory reduction.

news.summary: Bitcoin has hit an all-time high of $111,800 and is now facing a pivotal moment as long-term holders intensify profit-taking, according to Glassnode data. The initial surge was fueled by aggressive spot buying but is now experiencing sell pressure from previous market participants, testing the market's strength. Glassnode's analysis highlights key support levels at $103,700 and $95,600 that will likely determine if bullish momentum can continue. The report warns that with older investors selling off and support levels in focus, bulls are being challenged. The current cycle has seen significant profits for investors.
news.headline: Bitcoin's climb to $111,800 faces resistance as long-term holders start taking profits, testing the market's resilience.

news.summary: In Manaus, Brazil, on June 05, 2025, Brazil Potash Corp. disclosed that Adriano Espeschit is stepping down as President of Potássio do Brasil Ltda. - a subsidiary of the Company. The departure is set to occur in 30 days as per the agreed terms, allowing Espeschit to explore new ventures. Brazil Potash Corp. is a mineral exploration firm listed on the NYSE-American exchange under the ticker symbol GRO. The company focuses on critical mineral potash mining, notably through the Autazes Project.
news.headline: Brazil Potash Corp. announces President of subsidiary Resignation effective in 30 days. Adriano Espeschit stepping down.

news.summary: On June 4, 2025, Blue Moon Metals Inc. (TSXV: MOON; OTCQX: BMOOF) announced that the European Union Commission recognized its Nussir Project in Northern Norway as a Strategic Critical Raw Material Project. This designation was made under the 2023 EU Critical Raw Materials Act, with a total of 13 projects from outside the EU being added to the Strategic projects list. The goal of the CRMA is to enhance the availability of critical minerals and decrease reliance on China for the supply chain.
news.headline: Blue Moon Metals' Nussir Copper Project in Norway gets EU's strategic status under CRMA, part of 13 selected projects.

Two institutional investors have exercised these warrants. The operations were accomplished as a part of the uncommercial initiative by FNZ.
news.headline: CDPQ and Temasek exercise warrants from FNZ's $1.5B capital raises, leaving out employee shareholders.

news.summary: Unitas Funding, based in Cranford, N.J., has recently appointed John V. Santilli as Chief Production Officer. This move signifies a notable advancement in the company’s strategic expansion and dedication to pioneering private lending. Santilli will work closely with the executive team at Unitas to spearhead production strategy and implementation. His role is pivotal in the company’s efforts to reshape private lending by streamlining products and processes.

news.summary: B&W, based in Akron, Ohio, has confirmed the sale of its subsidiary, Diamond Power International, to Austria's ANDRITZ for a reported $177 million. The agreement was announced recently by the company, marking a significant milestone in its business operations. This move demonstrates B&W's strategic decision to streamline its assets and focus on core business activities. The sale of Diamond Power International underscores the ongoing trends of mergers and acquisitions in the global market, with companies reevaluating their portfolios to drive growth and efficiency.
news.headline: Babcock & Wilcox to sell Diamond Power International business to ANDRITZ for $177 million.

news.summary: In Barcelona, Spain, Wallbox (NYSE: WBX), a prominent player in electric vehicle (EV) charging and energy management solutions, revealed that it has recently concluded two private sales of the Company’s Class A ordinary shares, amassing a total of $15 million. The primary investment amounts to over $9 million and comes from the Government of Spain through the Spanish Society for Technological Transformation (SETT). This investment marks SETT's entrance into Wallbox’s shareholder group as part of the Company’s ongoing financing round that began in Februa.
news.headline: Wallbox secures $15M in private placement finacings, $9M from Govt of Spain. Leading EV charging solutions.

news.summary: Trane Technologies plc (NYSE:TT), a worldwide climate solutions provider, announced a quarterly dividend of $0.94 per ordinary share ($3.76 annualized). Shareholders of record as of September 5, 2025, will receive the payment on September 30, 2025. The company has increased its quarterly dividend by 77% since March 2020. Trane Technologies has a long history of paying consecutive quarterly cash dividends on common shares since 1919.
news.headline: Trane Technologies declared $0.94 quarterly dividend per share ($3.76 annualized) and raised quarterly dividend by 77%.

news.summary: K92 Mining has recently revealed plans to expand the near-mine infrastructure at the Dilatant Zone. Additionally, they have identified extensions to the high-grade zone and a possible new high-grade zone along the strike. This expansion project is significant for the company as it aims to tap into additional resources and bolster production. This development is expected to require an investment of around $XX million and has the potential to significantly increase the overall mineral reserves of the project area.
news.headline: K92 Mining expands near-mine infrastructure, high-grade zone extensions, and potential new zone along strike.

news.summary: Hyperscale Data, Inc., a diversified holding company listed on NYSE American as GPUS, revealed that starting July 1, 2025, the $PEPE meme coin will be a valid payment option on Bitnile.com. This platform is a sweepstakes-oriented social-casino website managed by Bitnile.com, Inc., an indirectly fully owned subsidiary of Hyperscale Data. The new payment method is set to enhance the user experience on Bitnile.com.
news.headline: $PEPE meme coin will be accepted as crypto payment on Bitnile.com on July 1, 2025, owned by Hyperscale Data.

news.summary: ImmuCell Corporation, listed on the Nasdaq as ICCC, a company specializing in animal health products for dairy and beef cattle, has shared news about its upcoming 2025 Annual Meeting of Stockholders. The meeting is scheduled to take place virtually on June 12, 2025, at 9:30 AM ET. This information was initially provided in the Proxy Statement filed with the SEC on April 24, 2025. The company focuses on developing, manufacturing, and marketing scientifically backed products to enhance the health and productivity of cattle.
news.headline: ImmuCell will hold virtual-only Annual Meeting of Stockholders on June 12, 2025, at 9:30 AM ET as per SEC filing.

news.summary: Vancouver, British Columbia, June 5, 2025 - A mining company known as Thesis Gold Inc. (TSXV: TAU, WKN: A3EP87, OTCQX: THSGF) has initiated the process of deploying teams for the upcoming 2025 exploration activities at its Lawyers-Ranch Project in British Columbia. This project is fully owned by Thesis and is situated in a highly productive area.
news.headline: Thesis Gold Inc. mobilizes crew for 2025 exploration at Lawyers-Ranch Project in BC. TSXV: TAU, WKN: A3EP87, OTCQX: THSGF.

news.summary: Enrollment has been finished for the Phase 3 ENSURE trials of Vidofludimus Calcium in Relapsing Multiple Sclerosis. The top-line data for these trials is anticipated by the end of 2026. Additionally, more data from the Phase 2 CALLIPER trial in Progressive Multiple Sclerosis has been released, reinforcing the positive top-line results previously disclosed. The total number of participants included in the ENSURE trials has reached a significant figure, showcasing promising progress in the research on this potential treatment for multiple sclerosis. The Phase 2 trial also contributed valuable insights into the effectiveness of the treatment in progressive MS cases. Stay tuned for further updates on this groundbreaking research.
news.headline: Immunic finishes enrolling for Phase 3 ENSURE trials in Relapsing MS; Phase 2 CALLIPER trial shows positive outcomes in Progressive MS. Top-line data due by end of 2026.

news.summary: On June 5, 2025, Vermilion Energy Inc. disclosed entering a definitive agreement to sell its United States assets for $120 million in cash. The company, listed on TSX as VET and NYSE as VET, expressed satisfaction with this development.
news.headline: Vermilion Energy Inc. sells US assets for $120 million, updates 2025 guidance.

news.summary: In 2025, 79% of businesses are expected to boost their usage of packet capture technology. This tool has proven to help reduce Mean Time to Repair (MTTR) and enhance resolution speeds by four times within an hour. VIAVI's comprehensive network and security solution will be displayed at booth 2643 during Cisco Live!, held from June 8th to 12th in San Diego, California. This event will take place in CHANDLER, Ariz.
news.headline: 79% of enterprises plan to increase packet capture use by 2025 for faster incident response and 4X resolution improvement. VIAVI's solution will be at Cisco Live! booth 2643, June 8-12.

news.summary: On June 5, 2025, Life Time Group Holdings, Inc. (NYSE: LTH) based in Chanhassen, Minnesota, reported that affiliates of Leonard Green & Partners, L.P. and TPG Inc. are planning to sell 20,000,000 shares of the company's stock. The offering will be conducted by the Selling Stockholders.
news.headline: Life Time begins secondary offering of 20 million shares of common stock by affiliates of Leonard Green & Partners and TPG Inc.

news.summary: Economist Craig Shapiro believes that the debt ceiling, a tool used by only the U.S. and Denmark, does not effectively control excessive government spending and instead leads to periodic political drama. Shapiro, a macro strategist at the Bear Traps Report, points out the significant role played by the Federal Reserve in the bond market. He argues that the Fed's interventions, such as providing liquidity during market volatility, have facilitated Washington's extravagant spending by ensuring a stable market for U.S. Treasuries. According to Shapiro, these actions by the Fed eliminate market discipline as policymakers rely on the Fed to support their fiscal decisions, leading to increased deficit spending. Recent data shows a concerning trend as the U.S. federal debt has nearly tripled since 2009, frequently hitting the borrowing limit set by Congress, as reported by the Council on Foreign Relations. Shapiro suggests that if the Fed reduces its involvement and allows the bond market to operate more freely, it could help address the issue of rising debt.
news.headline: Economist Shapiro argues that Fed interventions fuel U.S. spending with federal debt nearly tripling since 2009. Fed removes market discipline.

news.summary: In Chantilly, Virginia on June 05, 2025, Parsons Corporation (NYSE: PSN) revealed that the American Council of Engineering Companies (ACEC) bestowed a 2025 Honor Award upon the Deer Creek Sanitary Tunnel and Pump Station project in the Water and Wastewater category at the national Engineering Excellence Awards (EEA). This recognition signifies Parsons' dedication to providing innovative engineering solutions that tackle current issues and advocate for a sustainable future.
news.headline: Parsons wins Engineering Excellence Award for Deer Creek project; ACEC honors them in Water/Wastewater category.

news.summary: APS, a European fund manager, has made a significant investment of $3.4 million in tokenized real estate through MetaWealth. This transaction is notable as it represents the first instance of a direct institutional acquisition of tokenized assets that are accessible to retail investors.

news.summary: Achieving a significant milestone, Hithium, a prominent global energy storage technology firm, has recently conducted the world's pioneering large-scale fire test for its ∞Block 5MWh battery energy storage system in XIAMEN, China. The test marks a groundbreaking achievement for Hithium, setting a new safety standard in the industry. This innovative energy storage system excelled in all four rigorous open-door test challenges, showcasing its robust safety features. The successful completion of this test demonstrates Hithium's dedication to enhancing safety within the energy storage sector, establishing it as a trailblazer in the field.
news.headline: Hithium achieves milestone with first all open-door large-scale fire test for ∞Block 5MWh system in XIAMEN, China.
